<!-- generated by gitdocs -->
---
title: "Auto Install Package"
category: Implementation
last_updated: 2026-04-25
---

Overview
1. Auto-installs the `claude-code-cache-fix` npm package when missing so the extension can run the proxy server and pipeline.
2. Chooses the installer based on the user's toolchain (Volta preferred when detected).

How It Works
1. On activate, if `autoStartProxy` is enabled and package not present, the extension calls `installPackage('reason')`.
2. `detectInstallerForPackage()` picks `volta` vs `npm` by probing PATH / `npm` resolution.
3. `installPackage()` runs the chosen command (`volta install claude-code-cache-fix@latest` or `npm install -g claude-code-cache-fix@latest`), streaming output to the proxy channel and resetting internal caches (`_packageDir`) after install.
4. After install, `getPackageDir()` is used to locate the installed package and then the proxy lifecycle continues (patch check, spawn).

Configuration
- `claude-code-cache-fix.autoInstallInterceptor` (boolean, default: `true`)
  - When `true`, missing `claude-code-cache-fix` triggers an automatic install attempt on activate.
  - When `false`, the extension surfaces a warning prompting the user to run the install manually.

Source Files
- `extension.js` (functions: `detectInstallerForPackage`, `installPackage`, `getPackageDir`, references to `_packageDir`)
- `package.json` (provides the `autoInstallInterceptor` setting)

Related
- See also: ../auto-architecture/proxy-subsystem.md
